message:
fix(fcp-zapier): validate action_id as a single safe path component (resolves br-62aa3)

Resolves flywheel_connectors-62aa3 — [REVIEW-A9][P2] connector
security: action_id was interpolated directly into
/exposed/{action_id}/execute/, allowing a hostile invoke input to
alter the API path on the configured Zapier host.

**Previous behavior (path injection):**

`ZapierClient::execute_action` at client.rs:218 used
`format!("/exposed/{action_id}/execute/")` with no validation. The
action_id flows from invoke input via `require_str`, so any string
containing `/`, `?`, `#`, `.`, or percent-encoded equivalents could
re-route the POST to a different endpoint on the same host. Example:
`action_id = "id/../admin/users"` posts to
`/exposed/id/../admin/users/execute/`, which after path
normalization on the server side could land on
`/exposed/admin/users/execute/`.

**Fix:**

New `validate_action_id` helper enforces:

- Non-empty, ≤ 128 bytes (`MAX_ACTION_ID_LEN` cap matches the
  practical length of Zapier NLA action ids — short slugs and
  UUID-likes — and bounds payload size for the worst case).
- Per-byte allow-set: ASCII alphanumeric + `-` + `_` only.
  Implicitly rejects `/`, `?`, `#`, `%`, `.`, whitespace, null,
  control characters, and any non-ASCII byte (which could be
  Unicode-normalized to a path separator on the server).

`execute_action` calls `validate_action_id(action_id)?` before
the format!; rejection surfaces as `ZapierError::InvalidInput`,
which maps to `FcpError::InvalidRequest { code: 1003 }` for the
caller. The `ZapierClient` never makes the HTTP request for an
invalid action_id.

**Regression tests (8 new):**

- `validate_action_id_accepts_well_formed_ids`: covers the
  legitimate shapes (alphanumeric, dashed slug, underscored slug,
  UUID-like, single character).
- `validate_action_id_rejects_path_separator`: locks in rejection
  of the exact `id/../admin/delete` shape from the bead repro.
- `validate_action_id_rejects_dot_segment`: `.`, `..`, `foo.bar`
  all refused — guards against server-side path normalization
  surprises.
- `validate_action_id_rejects_query_and_fragment`: `?`, `#`, `&`
  cannot smuggle query parameters or fragments into the URL.
- `validate_action_id_rejects_percent_encoding`: `%2F`, `%2e%2e`
  refused — proxies / Zapier may decode these before routing.
- `validate_action_id_rejects_empty_and_whitespace`: `""`, `" "`,
  leading tab, trailing newline.
- `validate_action_id_rejects_oversized_payload`: a multi-MB
  all-alphanumeric payload (would otherwise pass the character
  check) is bounded by `MAX_ACTION_ID_LEN`.
- `validate_action_id_rejects_non_ascii`: `café`, zero-width
  space — Unicode normalization could surprise.

255 fcp-zapier tests passing (208 lib + 47 integration; +8 new
versus pre-fix). Same connector as br-898o4 (63493e6e) — both
defects shared the pattern of accepting attacker-controlled
input at a network boundary without validation.
